story.soThat: configuration can be easily changed without code modifications
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create comprehensive `.env.example` file (AC: 10.3.1) - Subtask 1.1: Document all environment variables from Epic 1-9 - Subtask 1.2: Add descriptive comments for each variable - Subtask 1.3: Include default/placeholder values
- Task 2: Create environment templates (AC: 10.3.2-10.3.4) - Subtask 2.1: Create `.env.development` template with local values - Subtask 2.2: Create `.env.test` template with test credentials - Subtask 2.3: Create `.env.production` template with secure placeholders
- Task 3: Update `.gitignore` (AC: 10.3.5) - Subtask 3.1: Add `.env.*` pattern to ignore all env files - Subtask 3.2: Ensure `.env.example` is NOT ignored (positive pattern)
- Task 4: Add README setup instructions (AC: 10.3.6) - Subtask 4.1: Add "Environment Setup" section - Subtask 4.2: Document copy command: `.env.example` → `.env.development` - Subtask 4.3: List required values to fill - Subtask 4.4: Add Docker Compose integration notes
- Task 5: Implement config validation (AC: 10.3.7) - Subtask 5.1: Add ConfigModule validation - Subtask 5.2: Check all required variables at startup - Subtask 5.3: Throw clear error for missing variables

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. `.env.example` comprehensive (all required variables documented) 2. `.env.development` template created 3. `.env.test` template created 4. `.env.production` template created (with secure value placeholders) 5. `.gitignore` updated: `.env.*` files ignored (except `.env.example`) 6. README.md: Setup instructions added - Copy `.env.example` → `.env.development` step - Fill required values instructions 7. Config validation: Missing variables cause error at startup

doc.title: Epic 10: Development Environment
doc.snippet: Environment variable management, config kolayca değiştirebilleyim. AC: .env.example comprehensive, .env.development/.env.test/.env.production templates, .gitignore update, README setup instructions, config validation.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts → Environment Variables Contract
doc.snippet: Multi-Environment Support: Development, test, and production templates. Security by Default: All .env* files except .env.example are gitignored. Validation Layer: Application validates all required variables at startup.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.section: Dependencies and Integrations → Environment Variable Dependencies
doc.snippet: Epic 1: DATABASE_URL. Epic 2: JWT_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION. Epic 4: AWS_REGION, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, S3_BUCKET. Epic 5: SENDGRID_API_KEY, MAIL_FROM, FONIVA_*.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: No secrets committed to git. .env* files in .gitignore (except .env.example). .env.example contains placeholder values only. README warns against committing real credentials.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Minimum Coverage: 70% enforced by CI/CD. Coverage Breakdown: Utilities 100%, Services 80%+, Controllers 70%+, Repositories 70%+.

code.reason: Application bootstrap and configuration validation entry point
Joi validation schema defining all required environment variables and their constraints
Application configuration service using @nestjs/config registerAs pattern
Docker Compose service definitions with environment variable injection using ${VARIABLE} syntax
Development environment values for Docker Compose (already implemented)

package.name: @nestjs/config
package.reason: NestJS configuration module for environment variable management

package.name: joi
package.reason: Joi validation library for environment variable schema validation

package.name: dotenv
package.reason: Environment variable loader for Node.js applications

constraints.constraint: All .env* files except .env.example must be in .gitignore. No secrets in git.
Application must validate all required environment variables at startup using Joi schema.
Support for development, test, and production environments with appropriate templates.
Environment variables must work with both Docker Compose (docker/.env) and application-level .env files.
Must include all environment variables from Epic 1-9 dependencies.

tests.standards: NestJS testing with Jest. Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Unit tests: 70%+ coverage, Integration tests: Separate test database via DATABASE_URL.

ideas.test: Verify .env.example exists and contains all required variables from env-validation.schema.ts with descriptive comments.
Verify .env.development, .env.test, and .env.production templates exist with appropriate placeholder values.
Verify .env.development, .env.test, .env.production are ignored by git, while .env.example is tracked.
Verify README.md contains Environment Setup section with copy command and required values list.
Start application without required environment variable and verify clear error message about missing variable.
